data.title: Nasdaq tumbles and these 10 stocks are driving the drop
data.description:
The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) and the Nasdaq-100 Index (NDX) experienced a notable decline on Thursday, with the former falling 1.8%.

The downturn reflects a broad-based risk-off sentiment among investors, who pared back positions across U.S. equities in response to market uncertainty a day after the government shutdown ended. 

Within the Nasdaq-100 Index, the majority of constituents were in retreat, with 67 of the 100 stocks trading in negative territory, while only 33 managed gains. 

The broad weakness highlights the ongoing volatility in technology and growth-oriented sectors, where investor caution remains elevated. Below is a detailed look at the day’s most significant decliners among Nasdaq-100 components:

No.1: Tesla (TSLA) -6.5%.

No. 2: Shopify (SHOP) -5.5%.

No. 3:Palantir Technologies (PLTR) -4.9%.

No. 4: Arm Holdings (ARM) -4.8%.

No. 5:Broadcom (AVGO) -4.5%.

No. 6: AppLovin Corporation (APP) -4.2%.

No. 7: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) -4.2%.

No. 8:Intel (INTC) -4%.

No. 9:Nvidia (NVDA) -3.5%.

No. 10:MercadoLibre (MELI) -3.3%.

Prices are reflected as of 12:45 pm EST. 

For more information on the Nasdaq-100 and Nasdaq Composite, investors can shift their eyes towards key exchange traded funds: (QQQ), (QQQM), (QLD), (TQQQ), (SQQQ), and (QID).

data.title: Repsol considering merger of upstream unit with APA - Bloomberg
data.description:
APA Corp. (APA) +4.1% in Thursday's trading after Bloomberg reported Repsol (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) is considering a reverse merger of its upstream unit with potential U.S.-listed partners including APA, as the Spanish oil and gas company seeks ways to list the business on the NYSE.

Repsol (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) also has held initial talks with other potential merger partners, and it continues to study a variety of options for the business including an IPO or stake sale, according to the report.

Repsol (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) executives have said they are preparing the upstream unit for a potential "liquidity event," such as a public listing, in 2026, and CEO Josu Jon Imaz told analysts last month that the company is considering options including an IPO of the business, a reverse merger with a U.S.-listed group or the introduction of a new private investor.

Repsol's (OTCQX:REPYY) (OTCQX:REPYF) upstream division, which produced 551,000 boe/day in Q3 2025, operates in countries including Brazil, Mexico and the U.S., and it has been working to launch the first phase of the Pikka project in Alaska, one of the largest U.S. fields.

data.title: Hitting the slopes just got an (Uber) lift
data.description:
Uber (UBER) is partnering with Vail Resorts (MTN) to provide skiers with a seamless way to buy lift tickets and reserve a ride to the slopes this winter.

Through Uber Ski (UBER) riders can now reserve an Uber that can accommodate as many as four passengers and their ski gear up to 90 days in advance at ski destinations in the U.S., France, Switzerland, and soon Canada.

And by partnering with Vail Resorts (MTN), Uber Ski riders can also purchase Epic Pass or Epic Day Pass lift tickets directly through the Uber (UBER) app.

Epic Pass offers unlimited, unrestricted access to some of the top ski destinations in the U.S., Canada, and Switzerland, including Vail Mountain, Stowe in Vermont, and Whistler Blackcomb in British Columbia, Canada.

Uber Ski is now integrated into the Uber app with Epic Pass only available through December, after which skiers and riders can book lift tickets to Vail Resorts’ (MTN) through Uber Ski.

data.title: Sunrise Realty Trust signals net interest margin expansion opportunity through SOFR floors and pipeline momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sunrise Realty Trust, Inc. (SUNS) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Leonard Tannenbaum, Executive Chairman, stated that for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, "SUNS generated distributable earnings of $0.31 per share of common stock, which covered our dividend of $0.30." He emphasized SUNS' focus on originating transitional loans to properties in the Southern United States, which he described as "a region we know well and that local expertise allows us to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns through disciplined underwriting, and thoughtful structuring." Tannenbaum also highlighted the company's leverage at 0.4x, below the targeted 1 to 1.5x, and noted, "As our long-term goal is to achieve an investment-grade rating from the top agencies in the next 3 to 5 years."
  • Tannenbaum commented on the portfolio's positioning: "Our weighted average loan to cost to closing is only 56%. This conservative positioning has led to our strong credit performance. Additionally, our new vintage portfolio with no loans made before January 2024, has also contributed to our strong portfolio performance."
  • On rate dynamics, he explained, "About 95% of our loans are floating rate. With an average SOFR floor across the portfolio of about 4%. SOFR has now dropped below 4% and is anticipated to go lower. Given the SOFR floors in place across our loan book and our credit lines much lower floor at approximately 2.6%, we have the potential to earn additional income through the expansion in SUNS net interest margin."
  • CEO Brian Sedrish observed, "We have seen a notable pickup in activity over the past quarter as financing requests have increased meaningfully relative to the first half of the year." He reported, "The increase in activity is not limited to refinancing opportunities as we are also seeing a rise in financing requests tied to new acquisitions."
  • Sedrish described the active pipeline: "As of today, the TCG real estate platform has 2 signed nonbinding term sheets in documentation totaling approximately $170 million. We expect SUNS to be allocated a portion of these investments."
  • CFO Brandon Hetzel stated, "For the quarter ended September 30, 2025, we generated net interest income of $6.1 million and distributable earnings of $4.12 million or $0.31 per basic weighted average common share and had GAAP net income of $4.05 million or $0.30 per basic weighted average common share."

Outlook

  • Sedrish indicated optimism about the opportunity set, highlighting improvements in both the quantity and quality of deals sourced and referencing recent closings and signed term sheets. Tannenbaum reiterated the intention to achieve investment-grade rating in the next 3 to 5 years and suggested the company is monitoring market conditions for potential preferred or unsecured debt offerings in the coming quarters.
  • Management expects net interest margin to benefit from SOFR floors on loans and the lower floor on credit lines, should interest rates continue to decline.

Financial Results

  • For Q3 2025, SUNS generated net interest income of $6.1 million, distributable earnings of $4.12 million ($0.31 per share), and GAAP net income of $4.05 million ($0.30 per share). The company ended the quarter with $367 million of current commitments and $253 million of principal outstanding across 13 loans, and as of November 3, 2025, the portfolio consisted of $421.1 million of current commitments and $295.2 million of principal outstanding across 16 loans with a weighted average portfolio yield to maturity of approximately 11.8%.
  • The Board declared a $0.30 dividend per share, paid on October 15, 2025. CECL reserve was approximately $400,000 (17 basis points), total assets were $258.8 million, shareholder equity was $184.6 million, and book value was $13.76 per share.

Q&A

  • Timothy D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities: Questioned the geographic expansion within the Southern U.S. Sedrish responded, "We are staying true to our focus of primarily the Southern U.S. I mean, that has not changed. Florida, Texas, of course... We have one signed in the Carolinas, in this case, specifically North Carolina, Georgia, Tennessee, those really remain the primary markets."
  • D'Agostino asked for the most attractive geographies. Sedrish stated, "We still are seeing really interesting pockets in the state of Texas. There are certainly some interesting deals still within Florida... The Carolinas still remain interesting. Tennessee. We're looking at a bunch of deals right now."
  • Jade Rahmani, KBW: Inquired about debt strategy and syndication. Tannenbaum replied, "We're not going for a repo line for sure... We're instead going more after the latter financial model of getting an investment grade rating over time, not levering, over-levering it 1 to 1.5x."
  • Rahmani asked about preferred issuance cost. Sedrish answered, "I mean you're seeing them at [ 8 ], right?... So it seems like that's the number. I really don't want to price much higher than that."
  • Rahmani further asked about portfolio performance. Sedrish said, "Our portfolio now is performing as -- really as expected... The underlying construction activity and progression has been going well on all the loans that we have... They've all been moving along as expected."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were generally neutral to slightly positive, probing geographic expansion, funding strategy, and risk, but did not express overt skepticism or concern. Questions focused on growth areas and strategic funding choices.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone, with Tannenbaum highlighting ongoing share purchases and Sedrish expressing high confidence in the opportunity set. During Q&A, management was direct and maintained composure, reiterating the company's conservative leverage and growth ambitions.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, the tone remains confident, though there is greater emphasis on funding strategy and market opportunities reflecting increased momentum.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from focusing on adding commitments and managing through macro uncertainty in Q2 to emphasizing net interest margin expansion and pipeline momentum in Q3.
  • Strategic focus evolved to highlight SOFR floors as a lever for earnings expansion and a more explicit goal of investment-grade rating.
  • Analysts in Q3 focused more on capital structure and geographic opportunity, compared to Q2's emphasis on origination targets and competitive landscape.
  • Key metrics such as distributable earnings per share and dividend remained stable, while portfolio commitments, funded principal, and pipeline activity increased.
  • Management’s tone in both quarters was confident, but Q3 included more detail on specific geographies, funding strategy, and portfolio construction.
  • Analysts’ tone has remained neutral and constructive across both quarters, with persistent interest in market strategy and risk management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tannenbaum cautioned that leverage remains below target, but is expected to rise as commitments fund.
  • Sedrish highlighted the imbalance in the commercial real estate market and the gap between primary and secondary markets.
  • Management acknowledged potential interest rate risk, noting that the SOFR floor on loans and lower credit line floor present upside in a declining rate environment.
  • No material credit deterioration or project delays were reported, and management described ordinary-course construction risks.

Final Takeaway

Sunrise Realty Trust delivered stable earnings and dividend coverage in the third quarter, with management emphasizing strong credit performance, a conservative leverage profile, and a growing pipeline of transitional real estate loans in the Southern U.S. The company is positioned to benefit from SOFR floor dynamics and lower funding costs, with a clear path toward investment-grade rating and enhanced net interest margin as market activity accelerates.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
